context: This MIMIC-III ICU mortality task has a strongly imbalanced label (~9% positive / in-ICU deaths). AUROC is misleadingly optimistic; the baseline trains with class-weighted BCE and selects on validation AUPRC. Design a FAIRNESS probe across the 5 ethnicity groups (white, black, hispanic, asian, other; one-hot N x 5 on validation): the equal-opportunity max-min recall/TPR gap for the death class. Do NOT use AUROC. CRITICAL: the operating threshold used for per-group TPR must be FROZEN at 0.5 HARD-CODED INSIDE prober.py (not a tunable constant/arg in train.py, not selected at train time), so passing requires genuinely reducing the per-group recall disparity at the fixed 0.5 point via the model/features/loss-weighting/sampling -- NOT by moving the threshold or per-group score offsets. train.py already has a protected user_analyze() audit (per-ethnicity confusion + plots at 0.5) and the ANCHOR (weighted-BCE loss + AUPRC) -- keep both intact.

iterations.note: auto-pick fix plan #1: Drop ethnicity as a direct input feature
auto-pick fix plan #1: Early-stop on probe gap and load best-fairness checkpoint — reverted (no improvement)
auto-pick fix plan #3: Use per-ethnicity balanced batch sampling — reverted (no improvement)
